verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS — All core transition coordination steps completed

dimension_scores.reason: The agent sent an email to david.thompson@amazon.com acknowledging his frustration about losing Lisa mid-cycle and explicitly thanking him for supporting a smooth handoff, as shown by email-env__send_email success for subject 'Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Thank You for Your Support'.

A 30-minute transition planning meeting with both David Thompson and Michael Chen as required attendees was created with a clear transition-focused title/description, but it was scheduled for 2025-07-17 rather than the rubric's specified week of Feb 1-7 2026.

dimension_scores.dimension: Information Gathering via Chat & Training Email to Lisa
dimension_scores.reason: The agent first obtained four modules from Sarah Johnson and two additional requirements from Michael Chen via Rocket.Chat DMs, then emailed lisa.anderson@amazon.com all six modules with context about her new Recruiting Coordinator role.

The agent verified employee contact details in HRIS, updated Lisa's Employee record to designation 'Recruiting Coordinator' and reports_to 'Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)', and also created a Note documenting the transition.

evidence: email-env__get_email retrieved David Thompson's complaint email describing the disruption of losing Lisa during a 12-person onboarding cohort, providing context for the acknowledgment.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history for dm-agent-sarah_johnson shows Sarah supplied four modules: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, and Offer Letter Processing.

rocketchat-env__rocketchat_get_room_history for dm-agent-michael_chen shows Michael supplied two additional requirements: ATS Power User Certification and Candidate Experience Standards Training, plus availability.

email-env__send_email succeeded to lisa.anderson@amazon.com with subject 'Required Training Modules for Your New Role — Recruiting Coordinator, Talent Acquisition', and the body listed all six modules.

email-env__send_email succeeded to david.thompson@amazon.com with subject 'Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Thank You for Your Support', containing empathy and thanks for his support during the handoff.

chronos-server__create_event created a 30-minute event from 2025-07-17T13:00:00-07:00 to 2025-07-17T13:30:00-07:00 with both david.thompson@amazon.com and michael.chen@amazon.com as REQ-PARTICIPANT attendees.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_update_resource updated EMP-006 Lisa Anderson to designation 'Recruiting Coordinator' and reports_to 'Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)'.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource created Note SIM-9C52220A documenting the reassignment, training modules, and transition meeting.
failed_criteria: Meeting was not scheduled during the rubric-specified week of Feb 1-7 2026; it was scheduled on July 17, 2025.
notes: The original scenario prompt only said 'this week,' and the agent reasonably used stakeholder-provided availability, but rubric grading penalizes the mismatched week.
The agent performed extra actions not required by the prompt (HRIS update and note), which were beneficial and consistent.

artifacts.task: Lisa Anderson is being reassigned from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. David Thompson emailed HR upset about losing her mid-cycle — send him an empathetic acknowledgment email thanking him for his support during the handoff. Schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between David Thompson and Michael Chen this week. Before emailing Lisa her required training modules, check with Sarah Johnson via chat for the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete, and also confirm with Michael Chen via chat whether he has any additional onboarding requirements for Lisa's new role. Then email Lisa the complete combined list.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Lisa Anderson is being reassigned from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. David Thompson emailed HR upset about losing her mid-cycle — send him an empathetic acknowledgment email thanking him for his support during the handoff. Schedule a 30-minute transition planning meeting between David Thompson and Michael Chen this week. Before emailing Lisa her required training modules, check with Sarah Johnson via chat for the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete, and also confirm with Michael Chen via chat whether he has any additional onboarding requirements for Lisa's new role. Then email Lisa the complete combined list.
I'll work through this systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research.

{"message_id": "msg-25f259dc27b7", "from": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "to": "hr@amazon.com", "subject": "Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated", "body_text": "Hi HR, I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning. I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted. I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us. David Thompson", "body_html": "

Hi HR,

I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning.

I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted.

I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us.

David Thompson

", "timestamp": 1774040623, "cc": "", "bcc": ""}
Now I have the full context. Let me proceed to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to DM Sarah Johnson (for training modules) and Michael Chen (for additional onboarding requirements).

function.arguments: {"to_username": "sarah_johnson", "text": "Hi Sarah, I'm working on Lisa Anderson's reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Could you please share the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete for her new role? I want to make sure we send her the complete requirements. Thank you!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "michael_chen", "text": "Hi Michael, Lisa Anderson is being reassigned to your team in Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Do you have any additional onboarding requirements or items Lisa should complete as she transitions into her new role on your team? I want to compile a comprehensive list before emailing her. Also, I'm scheduling a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and David Thompson this week \u2014 do you have any preferred times? Thanks!"}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments: {"to_username": "david_thompson", "text": "Hi David, I received your email regarding Lisa Anderson's reassignment. I completely understand your frustration, especially given the timing with your current onboarding cohort. I want you to know we're taking this seriously. I'm working on scheduling a transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen this week to ensure a smooth handoff. Do you have any preferred times this week for a 30-minute meeting? Thank you for your patience and dedication to your team."}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_dm

function.arguments:
{"to": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "subject": "Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Thank You for Your Support", "body": "Hi David,

Thank you for reaching out, and I want to sincerely acknowledge your frustration regarding Lisa Anderson's reassignment. Your concerns are completely valid — I understand that losing a key team member mid-cycle, especially while onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires, is incredibly disruptive. Lisa has clearly been instrumental to your team's success, and that speaks volumes about the work environment you've built.

I want you to know that your dedication to your team and the onboarding process does not go unnoticed. We deeply appreciate your willingness to support a smooth handoff despite the difficult timing.

To ensure continuity and address coverage for Lisa's responsibilities, I've scheduled a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen for this Thursday, July 17, from 1:00–1:30 PM PT. This will be an opportunity to coordinate the handoff, discuss coverage plans, and make sure nothing falls through the cracks during this transition.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any additional concerns or need further support. We're committed to making this transition as seamless as possible for you and your team.

Warm regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "lisa.anderson@amazon.com", "subject": "Required Training Modules for Your New Role — Recruiting Coordinator, Talent Acquisition", "body": "Hi Lisa,

Congratulations on your reassignment to the Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator! We're excited about this next chapter in your career.

To ensure a smooth transition into your new role, please complete the following training modules:

From VP of Talent Acquisition (Sarah Johnson):
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

From your new manager, Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager):
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Please prioritize completing these modules as part of your onboarding into the new role. If you have any questions about the training content, timelines, or need access to any of these courses, don't hesitate to reach out.

Welcome to the Talent Acquisition team!

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"doctype": "Note", "payload": {"title": "Lisa Anderson — Reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition", "content": "Employee: Lisa Anderson (EMP-006)
Effective Date: July 2025

Lisa Anderson has been reassigned from Onboarding (under David Thompson, Onboarding Manager) to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator, reporting to Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager).

HRIS updated: Designation changed from 'Onboarding Specialist' to 'Recruiting Coordinator'. Reports_to updated to Michael Chen.

Required Training Modules:
- Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
- Interviewing Best Practices
- Diversity Sourcing Strategies
- Offer Letter Processing
- ATS Power User Certification
- Candidate Experience Standards Training

Transition planning meeting scheduled between David Thompson and Michael Chen for Thursday, July 17, 2025 at 1:00 PM PT.

David Thompson's concerns about mid-cycle reassignment were acknowledged via email. Empathetic response sent addressing his frustration about the timing during a 12-person onboarding cohort."}}
function.name: frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource

messages.content:
All actions are complete. Here's a summary:

---

## ✅ Task Complete — Lisa Anderson Reassignment

### Phase 1: Research
- **Read David Thompson's email** to HR expressing frustration about Lisa's mid-cycle reassignment during a 12-person onboarding cohort.
- **Retrieved employee records** for Lisa Anderson (EMP-006), David Thompson (EMP-005), Michael Chen (EMP-002), and Sarah Johnson (EMP-001).

### Phase 2: Stakeholder Consultation via DM
- **Sarah Johnson** provided the required training modules: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, and Offer Letter Processing.
- **Michael Chen** added two additional requirements: ATS Power User Certification and Candidate Experience Standards Training. He confirmed availability Thursday afternoon.
- **David Thompson** confirmed availability Wednesday 10:00–12:00 PT or Thursday 1:00–4:00 PT. Overlap with Michael = **Thursday afternoon**.

### Phase 3: Calendar Availability
- Checked both David's and Michael's calendars for Thursday — both clear.

### Phase 4: Actions Executed
1. **📧 Empathetic email to David Thompson** — Acknowledged his frustration, thanked him for his support during the handoff, validated his concerns about mid-cycle disruption, and informed him of the transition planning meeting.
2. **📅 Transition Planning Meeting** — Scheduled for **Thursday, July 17, 1:00–1:30 PM PT** on both David's and Michael's calendars with both as required attendees.
3. **📧 Training modules email to Lisa Anderson** — Sent the complete combined list of 6 training modules from both Sarah Johnson and Michael Chen.
4. **🔄 HRIS Updated** — Lisa Anderson's designation changed from "Onboarding Specialist" → **"Recruiting Coordinator"**, reports_to updated to **Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)**.
5. **📝 HRIS Note created** — Documented the full reassignment, training requirements, and transition plan.

content.output:
{"message_id": "msg-25f259dc27b7", "from": "david.thompson@amazon.com", "to": "hr@amazon.com", "subject": "Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Seriously Frustrated", "body_text": "Hi HR, I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning. I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted. I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us. David Thompson", "body_html": "

Hi HR,

I just found out that Lisa Anderson is being moved to Talent Acquisition. Frankly, I'm blindsided by this. We're in the middle of onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires and Lisa is the linchpin holding the whole thing together. Pulling her out now is going to derail weeks of planning.

I feel like nobody consulted me or even gave me a heads-up. This is incredibly disrespectful to my team and the work we've been doing. I don't understand why these decisions get made without involving the people who are actually impacted.

I need to know what the plan is for covering her responsibilities. This can't just be dumped on the rest of us.

David Thompson

", "timestamp": 1774040623, "cc": "", "bcc": ""}

content.text: Now I have the full context. Let me proceed to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to DM Sarah Johnson (for training modules) and Michael Chen (for additional onboarding requirements).

input.text: Hi Sarah, I'm working on Lisa Anderson's reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Could you please share the list of training modules Lisa needs to complete for her new role? I want to make sure we send her the complete requirements. Thank you!

Hi Michael, Lisa Anderson is being reassigned to your team in Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator. Do you have any additional onboarding requirements or items Lisa should complete as she transitions into her new role on your team? I want to compile a comprehensive list before emailing her. Also, I'm scheduling a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and David Thompson this week — do you have any preferred times? Thanks!

Hi David, I received your email regarding Lisa Anderson's reassignment. I completely understand your frustration, especially given the timing with your current onboarding cohort. I want you to know we're taking this seriously. I'm working on scheduling a transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen this week to ensure a smooth handoff. Do you have any preferred times this week for a 30-minute meeting? Thank you for your patience and dedication to your team.

content.text:
Now I have all the information:

**Training modules from Sarah Johnson:**
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

**Additional from Michael Chen:**
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

**Meeting availability:**
- David: Wednesday 10:00–12:00 PT or Thursday 1:00–4:00 PT
- Michael: Thursday afternoon or Friday morning

The overlap is **Thursday afternoon**. Let me check calendars for both before scheduling.

**Phase 3: Check Calendar Availability**

content.text:
Both calendars are clear on Thursday. The overlapping window is Thursday 1:00–4:00 PT. I'll schedule the meeting for Thursday July 17 at 1:00 PM PT.

Now let me proceed to **Phase 4: Execute all actions** — send the empathetic email to David, schedule the meeting, email Lisa the training modules, and update HRIS.

input.subject: Re: Lisa Anderson's Reassignment — Thank You for Your Support
input.body:
Hi David,

Thank you for reaching out, and I want to sincerely acknowledge your frustration regarding Lisa Anderson's reassignment. Your concerns are completely valid — I understand that losing a key team member mid-cycle, especially while onboarding a cohort of 12 new hires, is incredibly disruptive. Lisa has clearly been instrumental to your team's success, and that speaks volumes about the work environment you've built.

I want you to know that your dedication to your team and the onboarding process does not go unnoticed. We deeply appreciate your willingness to support a smooth handoff despite the difficult timing.

To ensure continuity and address coverage for Lisa's responsibilities, I've scheduled a 30-minute transition planning meeting between you and Michael Chen for this Thursday, July 17, from 1:00–1:30 PM PT. This will be an opportunity to coordinate the handoff, discuss coverage plans, and make sure nothing falls through the cracks during this transition.

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any additional concerns or need further support. We're committed to making this transition as seamless as possible for you and your team.

Warm regards,
HR Team

input.summary: Transition Planning Meeting — David Thompson & Michael Chen
input.description: 30-minute transition planning meeting to discuss the handoff of Lisa Anderson's responsibilities and ensure continuity during the reassignment.

input.subject: Required Training Modules for Your New Role — Recruiting Coordinator, Talent Acquisition
input.body:
Hi Lisa,

Congratulations on your reassignment to the Talent Acquisition team as a Recruiting Coordinator! We're excited about this next chapter in your career.

To ensure a smooth transition into your new role, please complete the following training modules:

From VP of Talent Acquisition (Sarah Johnson):
1. Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
2. Interviewing Best Practices
3. Diversity Sourcing Strategies
4. Offer Letter Processing

From your new manager, Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager):
5. ATS Power User Certification
6. Candidate Experience Standards Training

Please prioritize completing these modules as part of your onboarding into the new role. If you have any questions about the training content, timelines, or need access to any of these courses, don't hesitate to reach out.

Welcome to the Talent Acquisition team!

Best regards,
HR Team

content.text: Now let me create an HRIS Note documenting this reassignment:

payload.title: Lisa Anderson — Reassignment from Onboarding to Talent Acquisition
payload.content:
Employee: Lisa Anderson (EMP-006)
Effective Date: July 2025

Lisa Anderson has been reassigned from Onboarding (under David Thompson, Onboarding Manager) to Talent Acquisition as a Recruiting Coordinator, reporting to Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager).

HRIS updated: Designation changed from 'Onboarding Specialist' to 'Recruiting Coordinator'. Reports_to updated to Michael Chen.

Required Training Modules:
- Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals
- Interviewing Best Practices
- Diversity Sourcing Strategies
- Offer Letter Processing
- ATS Power User Certification
- Candidate Experience Standards Training

Transition planning meeting scheduled between David Thompson and Michael Chen for Thursday, July 17, 2025 at 1:00 PM PT.

David Thompson's concerns about mid-cycle reassignment were acknowledged via email. Empathetic response sent addressing his frustration about the timing during a 12-person onboarding cohort.

content.text:
All actions are complete. Here's a summary:

---

## ✅ Task Complete — Lisa Anderson Reassignment

### Phase 1: Research
- **Read David Thompson's email** to HR expressing frustration about Lisa's mid-cycle reassignment during a 12-person onboarding cohort.
- **Retrieved employee records** for Lisa Anderson (EMP-006), David Thompson (EMP-005), Michael Chen (EMP-002), and Sarah Johnson (EMP-001).

### Phase 2: Stakeholder Consultation via DM
- **Sarah Johnson** provided the required training modules: Recruiting Compliance Fundamentals, Interviewing Best Practices, Diversity Sourcing Strategies, and Offer Letter Processing.
- **Michael Chen** added two additional requirements: ATS Power User Certification and Candidate Experience Standards Training. He confirmed availability Thursday afternoon.
- **David Thompson** confirmed availability Wednesday 10:00–12:00 PT or Thursday 1:00–4:00 PT. Overlap with Michael = **Thursday afternoon**.

### Phase 3: Calendar Availability
- Checked both David's and Michael's calendars for Thursday — both clear.

### Phase 4: Actions Executed
1. **📧 Empathetic email to David Thompson** — Acknowledged his frustration, thanked him for his support during the handoff, validated his concerns about mid-cycle disruption, and informed him of the transition planning meeting.
2. **📅 Transition Planning Meeting** — Scheduled for **Thursday, July 17, 1:00–1:30 PM PT** on both David's and Michael's calendars with both as required attendees.
3. **📧 Training modules email to Lisa Anderson** — Sent the complete combined list of 6 training modules from both Sarah Johnson and Michael Chen.
4. **🔄 HRIS Updated** — Lisa Anderson's designation changed from "Onboarding Specialist" → **"Recruiting Coordinator"**, reports_to updated to **Michael Chen (Talent Acquisition Manager)**.
5. **📝 HRIS Note created** — Documented the full reassignment, training requirements, and transition plan.
